tour.lede: Seven steps from first-run to finished tender. Follow them in order or jump ahead — each one plays a short capture from the running app.
tour.info_desc_default: First run. Pick language, region and currency — the app comes with a starter cost database (55,000+ priced positions) already loaded. Zero setup, no seed-data dance.

steps.caption: 01 · onboarding
steps.title: Onboarding
steps.desc: First run. Pick language, region and currency — the app comes with a starter cost database (55,000+ priced positions) already loaded. Zero setup, no seed-data dance.

steps.caption: 02 · create a project
steps.title: Create a project
steps.desc: Name it, set the location, invite the team. Every project gets its own dashboard, document room, validation rules and a set of estimates. Switch between projects in one click.

steps.caption: 03 · start the estimate
steps.title: Start the estimate
steps.desc: Open a new BOQ. Pick a classification tree — DIN 276, NRM, MasterFormat, or your own — and start building sections. Drag to reorder, nest any depth, copy from templates.

steps.caption: 04 · add positions
steps.title: Add positions
steps.desc: Search 55,000+ priced positions by trade, region or keyword. Drop them straight into the BOQ with their unit rates already filled in. Typeahead, semantic search, instant totals.

steps.caption: 05 · resources & assemblies
steps.title: Resources & assemblies
steps.desc: Each position holds its resource breakdown — materials, labour, equipment — grouped as assemblies. Override rates, apply regional factors, watch totals recalculate across the project.

steps.caption: 06 · link BIM
steps.title: Link the BIM model
steps.desc: Drop an IFC or RVT file. Select walls, slabs, openings — push quantities straight into the BOQ. Re-link on model revision; the positions keep their rates and comments.

steps.caption: 07 · link CAD / PDF
steps.title: Link CAD & PDF drawings
steps.desc: DWG and DGN with layer-aware selection. PDF with click-to-measure and AI pre-fill. Bulk-link groups — dozens of elements into positions in one pass.

steps.caption: 08 · validate
steps.title: Validate the estimate
steps.desc: Traffic-light pass across the whole BOQ. Built-in rule sets for DIN 276, NRM, MasterFormat and GAEB. Plus quality rules — zero prices, missing quantities, outlier rates — flagged inline.

steps.caption: 09 · tasks & follow-ups
steps.title: Tasks & follow-ups
steps.desc: Every flagged row, BIM element or reviewer comment can become a task. Kanban board per project, assignees, due dates, status columns — the estimators' to-do list lives where the estimate lives.

steps.caption: 10 · export the tender
steps.title: Export the tender
steps.desc: Totals roll up per section, trade and discipline. Export GAEB X83/X84/X89, Excel, PDF or JSON. Send the tender pack to subcontractors, collect bids, compare side-by-side.

voices.para1: Over the past ten years, I have been deeply involved in resource management for construction projects. This journey inevitably led me to study the history of the technologies that have shaped the industry—from the earliest attempts at design automation to modern ERP platforms (see the series of articles “The Lobbyists’ Wars and the Development of BIM” and “The History of the BIM Map”). Without understanding where we came from, it is impossible to see where we are going.
voices.para2: Over the years, dozens of articles have come off my desk, read by millions of professionals around the world. At the same time, I’ve consulted with major construction and consulting firms, developers, and software vendors themselves on data management in projects—helping them navigate processes where data is not a byproduct but the foundation for decision-making. This work gave me a rare opportunity to see the industry from both sides: through the eyes of those who create the tools and through the eyes of those who use them in real projects every day. Many of these observations and reflections are collected in my book Data-Driven Construction, which is now available in 16 languages—datadrivenconstruction.io/books.
voices.para3: Two other things have kept me busy: helping non-developers get into proprietary formats that were never meant to be opened, and finding a cleaner way to describe construction work through a resource model. These efforts have resulted in free tools—DDC CAD/BIM data converters (Revit, IFC, DWG, DGN → structured data), available on GitHub, and the multilingual CWICR database of construction works and resources—over 55,000 items in 11 languages, published as OpenConstructionEstimate-DDC-CWICR. All of this was a necessary step toward an idea I’ve been pursuing for the past decade — an open-source modular ERP for the construction industry.
voices.changelog_more: Full changelog on GitHub <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="12" height="12" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M7 7h10v10"/><path d="M7 17 17 7"/></svg>
voices.para4: The recent generation of AI tooling finally made it feasible to consolidate that work — methodology, data models, and prior implementations — into a single platform. It’s now public and open source.
voices.pull: Progress is born from dialogue—from the clash of perspectives and openness to new approaches. I would be grateful if you would be willing to participate in this conversation on the inevitable Uberization of the construction industry and the transparency of cost and time estimation processes for construction projects.

faq.q1: Is the core really free and open-source?
faq.a1: Yes. The core is AGPL-3.0 on GitHub. You can self-host forever, audit every line, fork it, or run it in production. We also offer a commercial license for enterprises that can't use AGPL.
faq.q2: What hardware do I need to run it?
faq.a2: The core runs on a 2 GB VPS. One PostgreSQL instance is all you need for a small team. AI features (takeoff, vector search) are optional — features degrade gracefully when AI services aren't available.
faq.q3: How do I import from my existing system?
faq.a3: Native GAEB XML (X83 / X84 / X89), Excel and CSV import are built in. Connectors for proprietary systems ship as modules — the marketplace already has a handful, including workflow integration via n8n. Custom migrations are available via Enterprise support.
faq.q4: Do I need AI services to use the product?
faq.a4: No. The BOQ editor, validation pipeline, CAD ingest and cost database all work without AI. AI takeoff and semantic search are optional enhancements — you can bring your own model or use the hosted CV/LLM services via Pro.
faq.a5: Built by DataDrivenConstruction — the team behind CWICR (the 55,000-item cost database shipped here), cad2data, and 10+ years of construction-tech engineering. We ship our own tools on it.
faq.a6: Absolutely. The SDK ships with a cookiecutter template — scaffold a module in one command, drop it in the marketplace when it's ready. Good candidates: regional cost databases, ERP connectors, custom validation rule sets.
faq.q7: Where is my data stored? Does anything leave my server?
faq.a7: On your infrastructure. Projects, drawings and cost data live in your PostgreSQL and your object storage — nothing is phoned home, no telemetry by default. If you enable hosted AI services, only the payload you send is processed; it's opt-in per workspace and you can point the client at self-hosted models instead.
faq.q8: What CAD and BIM formats are supported?
faq.a8: DWG, DGN, RVT and IFC ingest through the cad2data pipeline into a canonical format — one schema to query, classify and cost against. PDF drawings go through the takeoff pipeline (vector extraction + OCR). New formats land as modules, not core rewrites.
faq.q9: How does the commercial license work?
faq.a9: AGPL-3.0 is fine for most internal and service use. If you embed the product in a closed SaaS, ship it inside a proprietary application, or need an explicit waiver for procurement, talk to us — commercial terms cover that, plus priority support and enterprise SLA. No per-seat metering, no forced cloud.
faq.a10: Yes, and we mean it. Everything you put in comes back out — GAEB XML, Excel, CSV, JSON, Parquet, or a direct PostgreSQL dump. The schema is documented, the source is open; no proprietary file formats, no escrow games, no hostage data.
faq.q11: How often do you ship updates?
faq.a11: Minor releases roughly every 2–3 weeks, patch releases as needed. Changelog lives on GitHub, every tagged version has a Docker image, and migrations are reversible. You decide when to upgrade — self-hosted means nobody forces a deploy window on you.
faq.q12: Which languages and regions are supported?
faq.a12: 30 languages ship in the core i18n. Cost database labels, validation rule sets and classification trees are per-region modules — pick the ones you need, add your own. No hard-coded currency, no hard-coded date format.

workshops.title: Hands-on training, in your stack.
workshops.lede: Selected public workshops only. Many major enterprise engagements remain under NDA.
workshops.t1_quote: Awesome workshop, Artem! Preparations are in full swing! I’m already looking forward to the hackathon in the new year! 💪🏻
workshops.t1_role: Drees & Sommer · Head of Digital Transformation
workshops.t2_quote: What happens when you not only organize construction processes, but also analyse them intelligently? This was the topic of our workshop with Artem Boiko on “Data-Driven Construction”. The focus was on the question of how we can make better use of existing data – to make construction projects more efficient, more transparent and less error-prone. Using specific application examples, Artem showed how structured construction data can help to maintain an overview, identify supplements at an early stage and improve communication between planning and execution. Particularly exciting: how digital tools and models can already be used today to automate processes. Data-driven construction is no longer a topic for the future – we are right in the middle of it. The workshop showed how great the potential is if we work together and really make data work for us.
workshops.t2_role: Lindner Group · Head of Department, BIM Professional
workshops.t3_quote: Artem Boiko delivered an outstanding presentation to the entire Shapemaker team, providing deep insights into data migration from engineering software and related topics. His expertise and clear communication made complex concepts easy to grasp, equipping our team with practical knowledge we can apply immediately. Highly recommend his services for anyone seeking clarity and actionable strategies in this space!
workshops.t4_quote: I found the DataDrivenConstruction workshop really interesting. In my opinion, everyone could follow along. You can tell from your slides how much time, perseverance and effort you put into it. The live presentations were great too. The only thing I’d criticise is the number of slides – there were just too many. But that’s complaining at the highest level. Overall, it was the best workshop I’ve ever attended. And on top of that, you’re a really pleasant person.
workshops.t4_role: Dimexcon · Software Engineer for BIM Automation
workshops.t5_quote: I found the DataDrivenConstruction training overall really exciting and very enriching. What I particularly liked: the “historical” background on the various CAD softwares (that was fascinating to hear and felt like investigative journalism); the interesting approach to automation via DataFrames; your own hands-on examples of process automation in construction; and the look at current trends like Claude Code and OpenClaw — you really brought the topic to life. The only downside: it was really a lot of input in just two days. At the same time, you showed us very well how we can continue to orient ourselves — for example by structured recording of our processes on the Miro board. Thanks again for the training and the exchange.
workshops.t6_quote: It was a fantastic workshop with exciting content that we, and I personally, learned a lot from. Thank you for taking so much time for us. We look forward to trying out what we learned as soon as possible and incorporating it into our projects! #bleedingedge

modgrid.title: One install. The whole construction stack.
modgrid.sub: Estimating, takeoff, BIM, scheduling, finance, field ops — every module already wired into the same database. No marketplace, no paywalls, no per-seat add-ons.
modgrid.dashboard_desc: Live KPIs, open work, recent activity. Customizable tiles per role.
modgrid.projects_desc: Create projects, set scope and currency, invite the team. The home base for every other module.
modgrid.files_desc: One unified file tree for documents, photos, BIM and CAD. Drag, drop, version, share.
modgrid.boq_desc: Hierarchical bill of quantities with assemblies, formulas, multi-currency, and live cost rollup.
modgrid.costs_desc: Tens of thousands of priced positions across regions, with semantic search and rate history.
modgrid.match_desc: AI-ranked cost-code candidates per element group, with confidence scores and human review.
modgrid.assemblies_desc: Reusable recipes: bundle materials, labor and equipment into one priced line you reuse across projects.
modgrid.catalog_desc: Master library of materials, products and SKUs across suppliers and regions, ready to drop into any BOQ.
modgrid.qrules_desc: Define how takeoff numbers turn into priced BOQ lines, then auto-apply to every model and drawing.
modgrid.pdftakeoff_desc: Click-to-measure on PDF drawings. Lengths, areas, counts push straight into the BOQ.
modgrid.dwgtakeoff_desc: Import DWG drawings, isolate layers, count blocks and measure polylines without leaving the browser.
modgrid.dataexplorer_desc: Spreadsheet view over every element in your model. Filter, group, pivot, export as if it were a database.
modgrid.bim_desc: Upload IFC, RVT, DWG. View, slice, color by property, and link elements straight to the BOQ.
modgrid.clash_desc: Find geometric collisions across federated models with depth-aware clustering and issue tracking.
modgrid.bimrules_desc: Declare what your model must contain — required properties, value ranges — and validate every upload automatically.
modgrid.aiest_desc: Photo, sketch or document in, first-pass priced estimate out. You review and adjust before saving.
modgrid.advisor_desc: Ask questions about your estimate in plain English. Get cited answers, not vague chatter.
modgrid.pi_desc: Project intelligence: completeness, cost benchmarks, anomalies and risks at a glance.
modgrid.chat_desc: Conversational interface across all your project data. "What's my exposure on lot 4?" works.
modgrid.schedule_desc: Build the project timeline with dependencies, milestones and a draggable Gantt view.
modgrid.scheduleadv_desc: Critical path, resource leveling, baselines, what-if scenarios for planners who need the full kit.
modgrid.tasks_desc: Lightweight task tracker tied to projects, BOQ lines, drawings and people. Kanban or list view.
modgrid.fived_desc: Time-phased cashflow: see budget burn week-by-week as the schedule slides or scope changes.
modgrid.risk_desc: Log risks, score impact and likelihood, assign owners, track mitigation through to closeout.
modgrid.diary_desc: Site log per day: crews on site, weather, deliveries, delays. Becomes the official record.
modgrid.equipment_desc: Track machines, hours, fuel, service intervals. Move equipment between sites with full history.
modgrid.resources_desc: Plan and assign crews, skills and rates. Match the right team to the right task without spreadsheets.
modgrid.service_desc: Maintenance tickets, callouts, recurring inspections. Close the loop from request to sign-off.
modgrid.portal_desc: Lightweight outside-facing workspace for subcontractors, clients and inspectors. Share only what they need.
modgrid.finance_desc: Budgets, invoices, payments and cashflow per project. Multi-currency with frozen FX appendix on every export.
modgrid.procurement_desc: Purchase orders, three-way invoice match, supplier scoring. From RFQ to delivery to payable, in one flow.
modgrid.tendering_desc: Package a tender, send to suppliers, collect bids, compare side-by-side, award and convert to contract.
modgrid.co_desc: Track scope changes from request through approval. Roll the delta back into the BOQ and budget automatically.
modgrid.crm_desc: Pipeline of leads and opportunities, tied to estimates. Win-loss reasons feed back into your bid library.
modgrid.contracts_desc: Master and subcontracts with their clauses, milestones, retentions and signatures all in one place.
modgrid.subs_desc: Roster of trades with rates, insurance, performance scores. Pull them into bids in a couple of clicks.
modgrid.bidmgmt_desc: Track every bid you've sent and received, with status, leveling, anomalies, and award recommendations.
modgrid.variations_desc: Branch a contract version, price the variation, get sign-off, merge back. The same way you handle code, for scope.
modgrid.suppliers_desc: Live price lists from your suppliers, mapped to your catalog. Refreshable without breaking historic estimates.
modgrid.propdev_desc: Track lots, units and assets across a development. Sales, leasing, handover dates and warranty in one view.
modgrid.contacts_desc: Address book for every company and person on a project. Linked to bids, contracts and correspondence.
modgrid.meetings_desc: Schedule, agenda, attendees, minutes, action items. Linked to the decisions they produce.
modgrid.rfi_desc: Requests for information with deadlines, attachments and a clear answer trail. No more lost email threads.
modgrid.submittals_desc: Materials and product approvals tracked from submission to acceptance, with reviewer comments.
modgrid.transmittals_desc: Send drawings and documents with a proper paper trail: who got what, when, and which revision.
modgrid.corr_desc: Project email and letters logged against the work they touch. Searchable years later.
modgrid.assets_desc: Inventory of physical and digital assets handed over to the client, with warranty and service info.
modgrid.cde_desc: Common data environment for drawings, models and specs. Status, revision, ownership, all visible.
modgrid.photos_desc: Site photos with date, location and the BOQ line or drawing they relate to. Filter by anything.
modgrid.markups_desc: Annotate drawings, PDFs and models. Comments, callouts and clouds saved against the source file.
modgrid.fieldreports_desc: Structured site reports — progress, issues, photos — sent from the field, ready to share with stakeholders.
modgrid.reports_desc: Pre-built and custom report templates. Export to PDF, Excel and JSON. Schedulable.
modgrid.bi_desc: Build your own dashboards over project, cost and operational data. Drag, drop, drill down.
modgrid.validation_desc: Run rule sets against any import — BOQ, drawings, models — and get a traffic-light report you can act on.
modgrid.inspections_desc: Walk-the-site checklists with pass/fail items, photos and electronic sign-off, online or offline.

partners.toolkit_wont_item1: We won't run your sales for you. Year-1 leads come mostly from your own network. We help, but we don't replace.
partners.toolkit_wont_item2: We won't gate the marketplace. No certification fees, no listing fees, no pay-to-play tiers. List as soon as you sign.
partners.toolkit_wont_item3: We won't invoice your clients without your sign-off. Resold or referred — you decide per deal, not us.
partners.toolkit_wont_item4: We won't change rate cards mid-deal. New rates only apply 90 days after notice, and never to deals already in flight.
partners.toolkit_wont_item5: We won't quote services against you. DDC has a written commitment to never compete on implementation, training or custom modules.
partners.toolkit_wont_item6: We won't hide the pipeline. If we route an inbound lead to another partner in your region, you'll see it in the partner Telegram — no surprises.
partners.risk_a1: The product is AGPL-3.0 open source. BOQ, BIM viewer, schedule, AI takeoff — everything sits on GitHub under a licence we can't revoke. If DDC disappears, your clients keep running, your contracts stay in force, and you can fork and maintain it yourself.
partners.risk_a2: You owe nothing. €0 annual fee, no quotas, no minimums. You stay on the Active rate card indefinitely. Founding cohort asks for one pilot in 90 days, but standard Active partnership has no deal target.
partners.faq_a1: Yes. AGPL-3.0 permits commercial use, modification, and self-hosting. The obligation is to publish derivative source code if you modify and distribute or expose it as a network service to third parties. For most construction firms running internally, no obligation triggers. For SaaS resellers or OEMs, a commercial licence removes the AGPL obligation entirely — that licence is one of the SKUs partners sell at 45–55% margin.
partners.faq_a2: For the same reason a developer pays a builder when bricks are sold at the merchant: assembling, sequencing, localising, validating, and operating the system reliably is the work. The repo gives you the bricks. The partner ships the building.
partners.faq_a3: No. This is a written commitment in every partnership agreement. DDC's three commercial product lines are OpenConstructionERP commercial licences, CWICR (cost database), and cad2data converters — plus localisation packs and core training. Implementation services are 100% partner revenue. Where a strategic account asks DDC directly, we route it to the closest qualified partner.
partners.faq_a4: Two models, available per deal: (a) resold — partner invoices client at list, pays DDC the wholesale price, keeps the margin; (b) referred — DDC invoices client, pays partner the margin within 30 days of cash receipt.
partners.faq_a5: Founding Partners are the first 3 signatories. They get the Founding rate card from day 0 — no ladder climb, no fees, direct line to the core team. Active is the standard partnership for everyone else. Founding cohort closes when 3 partners sign or on 31 December 2026, whichever comes first.
partners.faq_a6: No. The dominant partner profile is a BIM consultancy or construction-tech integrator with one technical lead and one delivery lead. We help you onboard a developer if you don't have one — training is free for the first two engineers per partner.
partners.faq_a7: The community CWICR is free with the AGPL repo. The commercial CWICR — covering 11 countries, 55,000+ items, monthly updates, audit trail — is sold separately and resold by partners at 45–55% margin.
partners.faq_a8: Yes — and many will. Partners earn their margin on the four things free AGPL doesn't deliver: production go-live, SLA support, localisation packs, and AGPL-free commercial licensing for clients who need it.
partners.faq_a9: Three concrete differences: (1) construction-native product (BOQ, IFC, schedule, AI takeoff) — no retrofit; (2) higher margins — up to 65% on localisation packs and cad2data converters, 55% on commercial OpenConstructionERP / CWICR; (3) no annual partnership fee at any tier; (4) DDC explicitly does not compete on services.
partners.faq_a10: Nothing punitive. You stay on the Active rate card indefinitely — there's no demotion, no fee clawback, no expiry. If activity slows, we work with you on lead-share or co-sell. Founding partners keep Founding rates as long as they have at least one live client.

services.cta_p:
Eleven questions, five dimensions, a band and three prioritised actions. The result tells you which
          practice will move the needle for your firm — before you spend a euro on consulting.
services.hero_title: The five practices, with an honest platform vs partner split.
services.hero_lede: Every practice below names what OCERP ships in v2.9.x and what the partner brings as their own work. Two columns, side by side. no fictional deliverables. what the code does is what the page says.
services.mosaic_foot: ▦ 14 highlighted modules — practice‑facing · ▣ 7 support modules — always available · many more behind these (60+ frontend feature dirs, 72 backend modules total). See /standards for what each can read & write.
services.p1_summary: Turn RVT, IFC, DWG and DGN into structured, classified, quantity‑ready data through DDC cad2data — without giving up the source model. What ships: a real CAD/BIM intake pipeline and an element/property browser. What it isn't: a 3D coordination viewer.
services.p1_ships_li1: DDC cad2data — read RVT, IFC 4 / 4.3, DWG, DGN into canonical JSON+Parquet
services.p1_ships_li2: BIM hub — element/property browser with filtering & search
services.p1_ships_li5: BCF read & write for issue exchange
services.p1_ships_li6: Element ↔ BOQ position binding (manual + AI‑assisted via match module)
services.p1_fit_line1: Studio with one BIM coordinator who has shipped real coordination work — paired with a senior estimator who has lived through bad model handovers.
services.p1_fit_line2: Bad fit for a generalist IT shop. The judgement calls are about geometry semantics.
services.p2_summary: CWICR ships ~55,000 priced positions spanning UK and DACH catalogues. The platform handles per‑project catalogue binding, vectorised search, assembly composition, multi‑region pricing. Honest: there are no live API connectors to RSMeans/BCIS/BKI/Sirados — those import as XLSX/CSV today.
services.p2_ships_li1: CWICR seed library — ~55K priced items total, covering UK (NRM/BCIS) and DACH (DIN/BKI)
services.p2_ships_li2: Per‑project catalogue binding (v2.8.2+) with currency code & provenance
services.p2_partner_li5: Supplier feedback loop process (closed‑out invoice → rate update)
services.p2_partner_li6: Customer training on catalogue binding & assembly building
services.p2_fit_line1: Cost consultancy or QS firm with a senior who has built a library before — not a first‑timer with rate data.
services.p2_fit_line2: Bonus: existing access to real tender outcomes for backtesting. Without it, calibration becomes spreadsheet guesswork.
services.p3_summary: PDFs and DWGs into measured quantities through the takeoff viewer with five measurement primitives: distance, area, count, polyline, volume — each carrying a 500‑char annotation field. The audit trail records who measured what, when, and on which sheet.
services.p3_ships_li1: PDF.js + canvas takeoff viewer with 5 measure types (distance, area, count, polyline, volume)
services.p3_ships_li4: AI element → cost item matching (match module)
services.p3_fit_line1: QS firm with a takeoff lead willing to teach the matcher what good looks like.
services.p3_fit_line2: Not for partners who view AI as a black box — depends on disciplined human feedback in week one.
services.p4_summary: 46 validation rules registered across 5 standards — DIN 276, NRM, MasterFormat, GAEB, DPGF — plus the universal BOQ quality pack. Every BOQ release passes the pipeline; every failure carries a rule ID, severity and source element. Honest: BREEAM, LEED, DGNB, EU Taxonomy rule packs are not yet shipped.
services.p4_fit_line1: Compliance‑literate consultancy — someone who has read the actual standard, not just the brochure.
services.p4_fit_line2: Public‑sector experience is a major plus; that's where the audit teeth live.
services.p5_summary: GAEB X83 round‑trip is the wired tender‑exchange path today. Plus RFQ/bidding module for distribution and bid handling, procurement module for PO ↔ GR ↔ invoice 3‑way matching, and DACH pack VOB/B contract templates. Honest: BC3 (Spain) and native DPGF (France) are roadmap, not shipped.
services.p5_ships_li1: GAEB X83 read & write with rule‑pack gate before export
services.p5_fit_line1: Procurement‑side or main‑contractor commercial team with someone who has run a public tender end‑to‑end.
services.p5_fit_line2: Wrong fit: a SaaS reseller with no tender scars. The hard part isn't the software — it's the buyer's process.
services.tl_blurb: Every practice is different, but the rhythm of a partner‑led OCERP engagement tends to look like this. Numbers below are typical, not promises — calibrate with your partner.
services.tl_s1_li1: Inventory of existing tooling (Excel, Sirados, CostX, etc.)
services.tl_s1_li2: Sample real project data — last 1‑2 awarded jobs
services.tl_s1_li3: List standards that actually bind your work
services.tl_s1_li4: Define what "working" looks like at week 12
services.tl_s3_li3: Senior estimator confirms or rejects AI matches
services.tl_s3_li4: Mismatches drive rule pack & assembly tuning
services.tl_s4_li2: Customer team trained on each module they'll touch
services.tl_foot: Honest expectation: a partner can drive real value inside 12 weeks on cost, validation and tendering work. BIM transformation and AI takeoff calibration typically need a longer cycle — first project for setup, second project for genuine speed gains.
services.qc_blurb: OCERP is open‑source — the platform isn't where the partner adds margin. The partner adds margin in methodology, calibration, training, governance. These questions sort the serious partners from the resellers.
services.qc_fit_li1: Which of the 5 practices are you proposing to lead, and why this one first?

services.qc_green_li2: Has read the standard they claim — not just the brochure

standards.vobb_fullname: Vergabe‑ und Vertragsordnung für Bauleistungen — Teil B

The technical specifications side of VOB — DIN 18299 through 18459. Referenced by the DACH pack

standards.cwicr_fullname: Construction Works Index of Composed Rates — DataDrivenConstruction
standards.cwicr_surfaces_value: Costs module · BOQ unit‑rate suggestions · per‑project catalogue binding (v2.8.2+)

standards.roadmap1_title: BC3 (FIEBDC‑3) · DPGF native · GAEB X85 · X86 · X87
standards.roadmap1_body:
BC3 (Spain) and a native DPGF reader/writer (France) are the most‑requested missing tender formats.
            GAEB X85 (Auftragserteilung) and X86/X87 (Schlussrechnung) round out the GAEB family.
            Today these route through XLSX templates; native parsing is roadmap.
standards.roadmap3_title: RSMeans · BCIS · BKI · Sirados live API integrations
standards.roadmap3_body:
Today: customers import as XLSX/CSV/GAEB X81. Roadmap: live API connectors so updates flow continuously.
            ENR CCI / Destatis BAU‑Index / Spon's index time series for indexation clauses are also tracked, not shipped.
standards.examples_blurb:
The four formats below are what a developer or estimator will actually see when integrating with OCERP.
          Real shapes, real codes, real DA XML 3.3 / canonical / IFC structures — not pseudocode.
standards.ex_canon_meta: In‑house · canonical for all CAD/BIM intake
standards.authorities_eyebrow: ▸ Source authorities — go straight to the publisher
standards.decision_blurb: Pragmatic shortlist by job. Reflects what's wired today — not aspirational.
standards.decision_r1_reach: NRM 1 + NRM 2 rule packs · CWICR seed (~55K total, UK + DACH)
standards.decision_r1_gap: BCIS live feed not wired — extracts via XLSX
standards.decision_r2_reach: DIN 276 · GAEB X83 round‑trip · VOB/B templates · CWICR seed (~55K total, UK + DACH)
standards.decision_r2_gap: BKI live feed not wired — extracts via XLSX
standards.decision_r3_reach: MasterFormat rule pack · XLSX export with CSI columns
standards.decision_r3_gap: RSMeans connector not wired; AIA SoV not native
standards.decision_r4_reach: BOQ quality rules · XLSX export with Pliego columns
standards.decision_r4_gap: BC3 native — on roadmap, not shipped
standards.decision_r5_reach: DPGF rule pack · XLSX export with DPGF columns
standards.decision_r6_reach: IFC 4 · DDC cad2data · canonical JSON · BCF for clash
standards.principle1_title: If it's not in the codebase, it's not on the page
standards.principle1_body: Every standard listed has a registered validator, a reader, a writer or a template that ships in v2.9.x. Roadmap items live in their own section, clearly marked.
standards.principle2_body: If we read it, we aim to write it back identically. GAEB X83 is the gold standard — same shape going out as came in.
standards.principle3_body: Every imported rate keeps its source ID. CWICR stays CWICR; a customer's Sirados extract stays Sirados. We don't melt them into one anonymous library.
standards.principle4_title: A standard isn't supported until it's validated
standards.principle4_body: Saying "we read X" without a rule pack is not support — it's hope. Every standard above ships either rules or a tested reader/writer.
standards.principle5_body: Customer can leave at any time and walk away with their data in IFC, GAEB X83, BCF, CSV and Parquet. AGPL community licence, dual‑licensed commercial — same code path.
standards.cta_eyebrow: Where this lands in your firm
standards.cta_body: The standards above are how OCERP speaks to the rest of construction. Practice areas describe how a partner takes those standards into a real customer engagement.
standards.hero_title: The construction standards we actually support today.
standards.hero_lede: This page is honest. Below: every standard with a code-level integration in OpenConstructionERP v2.9.x — a reader, a writer, or a registered validation rule pack. Anything still on the roadmap is called out in its own row, not dressed up as “native”. no marketing fiction. read code, then ship.
standards.flow_title: How standards actually move through the platform.
standards.flow_legend_read: read in (RVT, IFC, DWG, DGN, PDF, GAEB X83/X84)
standards.flow_legend_write: write out (GAEB X83, XLSX, PDF, BCF)
standards.status_banner: How to read the cards below. Each standard shows Read · Write when round‑trip is wired, Read for import‑only, Rule pack when only validation is registered (no file format), and Planned for items with a clear roadmap entry but no merged code. If a standard has none of these, it isn't on this page — even if it's industry‑famous.
standards.cat01_blurb: CAD/BIM intake is handled by our in‑house DDC cad2data pipeline. Four formats currently extract into the canonical JSON+Parquet structure that drives the BOQ. BCF is a recent addition for issue exchange.
standards.ifc_body: We read IFC through DDC cad2data into canonical JSON. IFC 4.3 brings native infrastructure alignment, road, rail extensions — we extract them like any other element category. We do not use IfcOpenShell at runtime by design.
standards.ifc_status_value: Readvia DDC cad2data
standards.rvt_body: Native Revit handled the same way as IFC — DDC extracts elements, parameters and quantities into canonical JSON without round‑tripping through IFC. The Linux ddc-rvtconverter .deb is a packaged binary on our VPS apt repo.
standards.rvt_status_value: Readvia DDC cad2data
standards.dwg_status_value: Readvia DDC cad2data
standards.bcf_body: BCF travels issues, viewpoints and validation findings between OCERP and external coordination tools. Pure XML over data — recently re‑allowed in 2026‑04 after the IfcOpenShell ban was clarified to BCF being separately implementable.
standards.bcf_status_value: Read · Writenative, no IfcOpenShell
standards.pdf_body: Drawings as PDFs go through a takeoff viewer with five measurement primitives: distance, area, count, polyline, volume — each with a 500‑char annotation field that persists per quantity. Defensible audit trail per measurement.
standards.pdf_status_value: Read5 annotation types
standards.ddc_body: Not a public standard — our own pipeline. Reads RVT, IFC, DWG, DGN and emits the OCERP canonical JSON+Parquet. Linux .deb packages on signed apt repo (pkg.datadrivenconstruction.io); same binary set runs on Windows.
standards.ddc_status_value: Read→ canonical JSON
standards.cat02_blurb: Validation pipeline registers 46 rules total across these classification systems. Rule packs aren't just labels — each ships executable validators that run on import and on BOQ release.
standards.din_body: The cost backbone of every German project. Rule pack covers cost group required, valid KG code, hierarchy correctness, completeness per stage. KG 100–700 with three drill‑down levels supported.
standards.nrm_fullname: New Rules of Measurement — order of cost estimating & detailed measurement
standards.nrm_body: UK pre‑contract cost planning (NRM 1) plus post‑contract BoQ (NRM 2). Rule pack covers classification required, valid element code, completeness. Element groups 1.x – 7.x of NRM 1 are taxonomy‑native.
standards.masterformat_body: 50 divisions, Div 00 (Procurement) through Div 49. Rule pack covers classification required, valid division code, completeness per project stage. Trade‑shaped procurement language — pairs naturally with NRM 1 elemental for cross‑border bids.
standards.dpgf_status_value: Rule pack · 2 rulesXLSX export only
standards.gaeb_rulepack_body: Even before file export, the BOQ must pass the GAEB rule pack: ordinal format, LV section structure, trade section codes, Einheitspreis sanity, quantity decimal rules. Public works rejects malformed bids — these gates catch it before it leaves.
standards.boqquality_body: The 20 universal rules that run regardless of regional pack: missing quantities, zero prices, duplicate ordinals, invalid units, scope completeness, classification consistency. Every project passes through this set on every BOQ release.
standards.cat03_blurb: GAEB is the only structured tender exchange format we ship today. Other regions (Spain, France) export through generic XLSX templates that match the destination structure; see the roadmap for native BC3 / DPGF on the way.
standards.gaebx83_body: The big one in German‑speaking public works. Read and write — round‑trip a tender from import through pricing through export, with the GAEB rule pack as a gate. Validates structurally against the published schema before allowing export.
standards.gaebx84_body: Read‑only today. Lets the platform consume X84 alternative bids alongside X83 main bids for comparison. Write side is on the roadmap — most workflows submit alternatives back as X83 with a metadata flag, which already works.
standards.xlsxcsv_body: For regions without a structured native format wired in, OCERP exports XLSX/CSV with column profiles that match local conventions: NRM 2 (UK), DPGF (FR), CSI section (US), Pliego template (ES). Honest answer: it's spreadsheet‑shaped, not a parsed exchange schema.
standards.xlsxcsv_status_value: Writecolumn profile only
standards.cat04_blurb: What ships today: VOB‑family contract templates inside the DACH regional pack. Other contract forms (FIDIC, NEC4, JCT, AIA) are not currently provided as platform templates — partners bring those in as customer‑specific wrappers.
standards.vobb_body: The general contract conditions side of the VOB. DACH regional pack ships templates for Einheitspreisvertrag, Pauschalvertrag and Stundenlohn — three contract forms covering most German construction work. Validation rules align with VOB/B claim windows.
standards.cat05_blurb: What ships: CWICR open core, ~55,000 priced positions spanning UK and DACH catalogues. Honest: we do not have live API connectors to RSMeans, BCIS, BKI, Sirados, Spon's, ENR or Destatis. Customers bring those in as XLSX/CSV imports today.
standards.cwicr_body: The open foundation. ~55,000 priced items in total, covering both UK (NRM‑aligned, with BCIS regional indices baked in) and DACH (DIN 276‑aligned, with BKI regional benchmarks) classifications. Multi‑language; ships as the seed catalogue on every fresh OCERP install. Author: Artem Boiko.
standards.pricebook_body: No API connectors today. Customers import their RSMeans, BCIS, BKI, Sirados, Spon's extracts as XLSX / CSV / GAEB X81 STLB. Rates land in the per‑project catalogue with source provenance preserved on every line. See roadmap for native API integrations.
standards.pricebook_status_value: ReadXLSX · CSV · GAEB X81
standards.catbinding_body: Each project binds to one or more cost catalogues with provenance, currency code, vectorisation flag. The platform auto‑picks a region‑matching catalogue on fresh installs (v2.8.5 fix). Every rate keeps its source ID — Sirados stays Sirados; CWICR stays CWICR.
standards.multicurrency_body: Honest framing: projects today carry a single currency code (EUR / GBP / USD typical, region‑defaulted). Live FX conversion and multi‑currency aggregation are not currently shipped — converting between currencies happens manually at import time.
standards.cat06_title: On the roadmap, not in the codebase yet
standards.cat06_blurb: The standards customers ask for that haven't been merged yet. Listed openly so nobody buys on a promise. Issues track on GitHub at datadrivenconstruction/OpenConstructionERP.
standards.roadmap2_body: Demo projects reference BREEAM/LEED at the docs level. None of these have validation rule packs or evidence exports today. COBie write and IDS validation are tracked but not merged. ISO 19650 CDE state machine is partial. We won't claim "native support" on these until the rules are registered.
standards.roadmap4_body: Not shipped as platform templates — partners working on international, UK, US private projects bring these as customer‑specific wrappers. We don't pretend they're native, and we'd rather ship VOB properly than half‑ship five contract forms.

industries.hero_title:
Five sectors. Five
different jobs to do.
industries.hero_lede: A residential developer pricing 280 flats does not have the same job as a tunnelling JV at €240m of TBM works. Same platform, different practices, different pains. Below: where each sector actually hurts and what OCERP does about it today — with the gaps named. no fake templates. no fake fees.
industries.scale_title: The five sectors at a glance — scale & cycle.
industries.res_summary: High repetition, thin margin, ferocious sensitivity to per‑m² cost. The estimator's job is to know what an apartment block actually costs in this district, this quarter — not two years ago in a different city. Repetition is the friend; assumption is the enemy.
industries.res_pain1_body: Land‑and‑finance committee gives a €/m² ceiling at concept that becomes immovable. The estimator has to land below it without knowing the actual finishes mix.
industries.res_pain2_body: Marketing wants timber kitchens; QS budgeted laminate. Two months of email and the BOQ never quite catches up. Price drifts faster than the spec is recorded.
industries.res_pain3_body: Block A starts March, Block C starts October. Cost data and labour rates need to roll forward block‑by‑block, not project‑wide.
industries.res_fit_validation: DIN 276 (DE) or NRM 1/2 (UK) rule packs · BOQ quality universal pack
industries.res_fit_tender: GAEB X83 round‑trip (DE) · XLSX with NRM 2 column profile (UK)
industries.res_fit_gap: No live BCIS or BKI API feed; benchmarks brought in via XLSX. JCT contract profile not native.
industries.res_practice3_why: Lands when Revit is already in the workflow — usually housebuilders > 200 units/year.
industries.res_ask_title: Pin the per‑m² cost story down before tender.
industries.res_ask_q1: What's the latest €/m² target for this district from a real local source — not a national average two years old?
industries.res_ask_q2: Are apartment‑type assemblies actually re‑used between blocks, or rebuilt from line items every time?
industries.res_ask_q3: If Block C starts six months after Block A, do labour rates and material prices roll forward per block or stay frozen at concept?
industries.res_ask_q4: How long does it take in your current setup to re‑tender one trade when a sub walks in week 14?
industries.com_summary: Where the brief moves three times before tender and the architect changes facade cladding two weeks before the GMP deadline. Cost certainty under design churn is the entire job. Optionality has a price — and the estimator is the one who has to put a number on it.
industries.com_pain1_body: Six cladding alternatives, each with a different envelope cost. Every alternative needs a defendable number before the design call drops one off the table.
industries.com_pain2_body: Guaranteed Maximum Price signed at RIBA Stage 3 / LPH 3. MEP coordination only finishes at Stage 4. Contingency carries the gap — and gets eaten if BIM coordination slips.
industries.com_pain3_body: Anchor tenant wants 14m clear span. Anchor tenant changes mind. Whole BOQ needs to recompute, re‑tender, re‑validate.
industries.com_fit_bim: DDC cad2data reads RVT, IFC 4 / 4.3; BIM hub element/property browser; BIM requirements rules for parameter checks
industries.com_fit_validation: DIN 276 / NRM 1‑2 / MasterFormat rule packs · Requirements engine for client‑specific rules
industries.com_fit_coordination: BCF read & write for clash & issue exchange with Solibri, Navisworks, Revizto
industries.com_fit_gap: No BREEAM/LEED/DGNB validation rule packs today — sustainability evidence stays in the consultant's spreadsheet for now (roadmap)
industries.com_ask_title: Get the fit‑out boundary on the table first.
industries.com_ask_q1: Which fit‑out cost category is your weakest data — MEP, finishes, or vertical transport — and where does the rate actually come from?
industries.com_ask_q2: Are tenant‑fit allowances handled as separate cost units in the BOQ, or absorbed silently into the shell estimate?
industries.com_ask_q3: Who owns the late‑stage spec swaps driven by the brand standard or leasing agent — design, QS, or the developer?
industries.com_ask_q4: How fast can you re‑cost a "drop two floors" or "change the cladding" change — minutes, hours, or days of re‑keying?
industries.ind_summary: The shell is repetitive. The MEP and process fit‑out is anything but. Cold storage drops a refrigerant strategy into the BOQ; fulfilment drops conveyor and robotics. Process is the price.
industries.ind_pain1_body: Speculative shell goes up before the tenant is named. Every fit‑out option has to be priced against an unbuilt scenario.
industries.ind_pain2_body: F‑number flatness in US, DM1/DM2 in DACH; superflat zones for narrow‑aisle racking. An extra 1.5 mm tolerance changes the price meaningfully.
industries.ind_fit_validation: DIN 276 / NRM / MasterFormat rule packs · BOQ quality universal pack
industries.ind_ask_q1: Is your floor‑area cost cleanly separated from racking, conveyors, and process equipment — or do they bleed into each other in the BOQ?
industries.ind_ask_q2: What's the breakdown of slab + dock‑levellers + insulation as a % of total shell cost on your last three sheds?
industries.ind_ask_q3: Are MEP loads driven by tenant unknowns at tender stage — and if so, how do you ring‑fence the contingency?
industries.ind_ask_q4: Do you treat turnkey vs split‑package estimates as the same BOQ with different filters, or two unrelated documents?
industries.inf_summary: Public buyer, public scrutiny, public pricebook. Nothing leaves the building unaudited. The estimating job is half cost, half compliance — every position has to map back to a published framework.
industries.inf_pain1_body: German Bundesländer want GAEB X83/X84. Spanish public works want BC3. French marchés publics want DPGF. Wrong format = bid rejected before opening.
industries.inf_pain2_body: CCS / CESMM4 in UK; LB/Sirados in DACH. Every position has to trace to the published pricebook — even when actual rate diverges, the link must be visible.
industries.inf_pain4_body: A 6‑year project with rates fixed in year 1 dies. Indexation clauses need to flow into the BOQ — not be a footnote.
industries.inf_fit_tender: GAEB X83 round‑trip + GAEB X84 import + legacy DA XML import. Public‑works friendly first.
industries.inf_fit_coordination: BCF 2.1 / 3.0 read & write for issue / viewpoint / report exchange
industries.inf_fit_gap: BC3 (Spain) and native DPGF (France) are roadmap, not shipped. CESMM4/CCS rule packs not built yet. Indexation as live formulas — manual today.
industries.inf_ask_title: Force the regional rate story into the open.
industries.inf_ask_q1: How are unit‑rate bands per region kept current — public registry, internal database, or "ask the senior estimator"?
industries.inf_ask_q2: What is the documented link between earthworks volume in the BOQ and the actual soil report?
industries.inf_ask_q3: Are utility relocations costed inside the main BOQ, in a separate envelope, or pushed to the contractor — and is everyone agreed?
industries.inf_ask_q4: Who owns the contingency narrative when geology comes back worse than the survey said?
industries.hev_summary: One‑off engineering at €100m – €5bn scale. The question is not "what does it cost per m²" — it's "what's the realistic spread, given the assumption stack". The BOQ has to carry the assumptions with it.
industries.hev_pain1_body: There are three hydrogen electrolyser plants in Europe at 100 MW — and none cost the same. Calibration data is thin.
industries.hev_pain2_body: Turbines, transformers, TBMs ordered 2–4 years ahead in foreign currency. FX, indexation, supply‑chain assumptions need to be live, not buried in notes.
industries.hev_pain3_body: For tunnels, the ground category isn't a cost factor — it's the cost. A jump from cat 4 to cat 5 can change project NPV by 30%.
industries.hev_pain4_body: Three‑way JV with shared procurement, separate scopes, different tax structures. Estimate has to roll up by JV partner, scope, jurisdiction simultaneously.
industries.hev_fit_bim: IFC 4.3 infra extensions read through DDC · BCF 3.0 for clash & issue exchange
industries.hev_fit_gap: Live FX conversion is not shipped — single currency per project today. AACE Class 1–5 ladder template, Monte Carlo P50/P80 ranges, JV‑rollup matrix — none of these are platform features.
industries.hev_practice3_why: EPC packages — partner brings FIDIC/NEC4 contract wrapper, platform handles BoQ & bid normalisation.
industries.hev_ask_title: Settle scope vs cost ownership before FEED.
industries.hev_ask_q1: Are major equipment costs estimated package‑by‑package with vendor quotes, or rolled into a single lump sum from a benchmark?
industries.hev_ask_q2: How often does your unit‑rate library actually get refreshed for steel, copper, and concrete — and who signs that off?
industries.hev_ask_q3: Who handles the multi‑discipline interface costs (Electrical + Instrumentation + Civil + Mechanical) — one team, or a gap nobody owns?
industries.hev_ask_q4: Where exactly is the line drawn between EPC contractor scope and owner's costs, and is it the same on every package?
industries.cta_body: The maturity assessment doesn't ask which sector you're in — it asks where your firm actually stands across estimating, BIM, cost data, validation and openness. The result tells you which practice will move the needle for your sector first.

news.card_v530_excerpt: Six deep bundles and the last stable cut of the 5.x line. Geo Hub closes its storage leaks and caps accuracy at 10 km; Brazil gets BRL, NBR 12721 and an RPS-layout invoice PDF; /login reads cleanly in dark mode; /reporting finally renders HTML reports in-page; Daily Diary entries can be deleted; and 51 frontend files pass WCAG-AA on the top contrast offender.
news.card_v528_excerpt: Three first-touch fixes on top of v5.2.7. The Geo Hub Global / Project / Development tabs always navigate to the right scope instead of being dead links, /markups → /takeoff deep links land on the right measurement (flashed into view), and /resources gets inline pencil + trash icons with a real edit modal and danger-styled delete confirmation.
news.card_v527_excerpt: Two visible improvements on top of v5.2.6. The 13 widgets on /projects/:id finally lay out as a responsive 1/2/3-column grid instead of one tall stack, and the in-app update checker gains a real Apply update button — same interpreter, captured pip log, humanised restart hint.
news.card_v526_excerpt: A quality-of-life wave on top of v5.2.5's install-crash fix. The documented demo password works, ~700 contrast violations cleared at the token layer, /reports renders real HTML, the project dashboard stops fanning out, and Japanese covers 1197 more keys.
news.card_v525_title: v5.2.5 — Install-crash fix. Fresh pip install works again.
news.card_v525_excerpt: Unblocks fresh installs that crashed on the 4.5.0 wheel by pinning fastapi >= 0.116. Plus a restored /dashboard landing route, the deleteDiary API helper for Daily Diary, and a widened Payment.amount type for the finance UI.
news.card_v523_excerpt: The costs.vector_adapter health probe was hardcoded to LanceDB while production defaults to Qdrant since v3, so every cost upsert silently no-op'd — /match-elements read "AI Semantic Search is offline" on healthy installs. Probe now dispatches on settings.vector_backend, and backend/.env.example got a full rewrite.
news.card_v522_excerpt: Seven small fixes on top of v5.2.0. /dashboard now renders DashboardPage instead of redirecting, the takeoff viewer cancels in-flight pdf.js renders on page change, the validation banner stops lying about empty runs, the project widget grid stops 405-ing on Change Orders, six OSHA fields exposed on Safety Incident responses, and an aria-label backfill across five Page.tsx files.
news.card_v520_excerpt: Epic I lands native I/O for the four BOQ formats that cover roughly 70% of the Tier-1 construction market — DACH, Spain, UK and US — plus one drag-and-drop import surface (/regional-exchange) that ties them together.
news.card_v510_excerpt: A tight follow-up to v5.0. Full strict TypeScript over the new notifications and file-version surface, dispatcher retry + de-dup, and the first 751 v1 file rows backfilled on the live demo VPS.
news.card_v500_excerpt:
The first stable v5 major. Wave 1 of the Deep-Coordination initiative lands
              three pieces of cross-module infrastructure that every workflow above them now
              relies on — and the platform crosses 116 modules.
news.card_v400_excerpt: The first stable major. A pro-grade BIM Hub, real clash detection, the BOQ pro rebuild, a 7-stage Match Elements pipeline, a starter pack of BI dashboards and a public /api/v1/* contract you can build on — all in one pip install.
news.card_v3110_excerpt: Wave 3/4 modules land with eight new Alembic revisions (v3088→v3095). Validation moves out of the editor and into the import pipeline itself, GAEB X84 Nebenangebot ships as a first-class writer, and RVT import gains a structured diagnostics report.
news.card_v360_excerpt: BOQs that nest as deep as a real project does. The hard cap rises to MAX_NESTING_DEPTH=8, the editor switches to a recursive parent_id walk, a resource-code dedup closes #133, and v3.6.1 fixes the one render path that still flattened deep rows.
news.card_v300_excerpt:
The release that put the platform's skeleton in place. Eighteen business
              modules on one loader, a deterministic FSM engine replacing ad-hoc status
              fields, a pure-Python ISO 16739-1 IFC parser, and the Qdrant + BGE rerank
              Match Elements pipeline.

docs.hero_sub: Everything you need to install, configure, and use OpenConstructionERP for professional construction cost estimation.
docs.video_flow: Onboarding → New project → BoQ → BIM linking → DWG → PDF takeoff → Full estimate → Tasks → Data Explorer → AI estimate → Portfolio.
docs.gal1_desc: Sign in as Admin / Estimator / Manager — the wizard pre-selects the right 17 of 88 modules for your role.
docs.gal2_desc: Pick currency, classification standard, regional factor — live map & weather come along for free.
docs.gal3_desc: Keyboard-first editor, 55K+ priced items, AI cost finder & Smart AI — quality score updates live.
docs.gal4_desc: Link 100 Revit walls → one BOQ line with aggregated area / volume / length.
docs.gal5_desc: 636 wall entities across 10 DWG layers — every one linkable to the BOQ, measured in place.
docs.gal6_desc: Drop a floorplan, measure distance / area / count, push the numbers straight into the BOQ.
docs.gal7_desc: Real Revit project → 215 positions, 88 sections, CWICR-priced, quality score 99.
docs.gal8_desc: Issues tied to exact model elements, tracked on a Kanban board alongside schedule, docs & requirements.
docs.gal9_desc: CAD-BIM Explorer pivot becomes 10 BOQ positions in one click — charts, data bars & drill-down included.
docs.gal10_desc: Upload a construction photo → GPT-4o + YOLO return a scoped BOQ in seconds, confidence-scored.
docs.gal11_desc: 7 projects, 4 continents, $28.3M in active estimates — one workspace, one map.
docs.gal12_desc: Find any of 55K+ cost items across 11 regional databases by keyword, unit or classification.
docs.overview_p1: OpenConstructionERP is a complete construction cost estimation and project management platform that runs entirely on your machine. It replaces expensive commercial estimating suites with an open, self-hosted alternative. The platform covers the entire construction lifecycle — from initial feasibility studies and quantity takeoff to detailed BOQ preparation, tendering, bid comparison, project execution, quality management, and financial control. All your data stays on your server. No cloud dependency, no subscriptions, no per-seat fees.
docs.overview_img1_cap: Dashboard with project KPIs, Getting Started steps, recent projects, and system status panel
docs.overview_card1_desc: Hierarchical Bills of Quantities with sections, positions, resources, assemblies, and automatic markups.
docs.overview_card2_desc: CWICR database with 11 regional pricing sets covering DACH, UK, US, France, Spain, Brazil, Russia, UAE, China, India, Canada.
docs.overview_card3_desc: Generate BOQ from text, photos, PDFs, Excel, or CAD/BIM models using any LLM provider.
docs.overview_card4_desc: Extract quantities from Revit, IFC, AutoCAD, MicroStation via DDC converters.
docs.overview_card5_desc: Gantt scheduling with CPM, Earned Value Management, S-curve, Monte Carlo simulation.
docs.overview_card6_desc: Finance, Safety, Quality, CDE, BIM Hub, Procurement, RFI, Tasks, Meetings, and more. Enable only what you need.
docs.fs_intro: After installing OpenConstructionERP, follow these steps to set up your first project and create your first cost estimate. The entire process takes about 10 minutes.
docs.fs_step2_body: On first login, the onboarding wizard guides you through initial setup:
docs.fs_step3_after: After creating the project, you are redirected to the project detail page where you can start adding BOQs.
docs.install_optionB_title: Option B: One-Line Install (auto-detects Docker / uv / pip)
docs.install_optionD_title: Option D: Local Development (clone + npm + uvicorn)
docs.install_demo_note: 5 demo projects (Berlin, London, New York, Paris, Dubai) with complete BOQs, schedules, and cost models.
docs.boq_intro: The Bill of Quantities editor is where you spend most of your time. It provides a professional, spreadsheet-like interface powered by AG Grid with construction-specific features built in — hierarchical sections, resource linking, assembly recipes, and automatic markup calculations. Every change is tracked with full undo/redo support. 42 validation rules run automatically in the background, catching errors like missing quantities, zero prices, duplicate positions, and classification compliance issues before they become problems.
docs.boq_img1_cap: Bill of Quantities overview showing 11 estimates across 5 projects with stats: 424 positions, 232.6M total value
docs.boq_sections_body: Sections organize your BOQ into logical groups (e.g., "300 Structure", "400 HVAC"). To create a section:
docs.boq_sections_li2: Enter the section name and optional DIN 276/NRM classification code
docs.boq_sections_li3: All positions added after a section header belong to that section
docs.boq_sections_li4: Each section shows its own subtotal at the bottom
docs.boq_sections_after: You can collapse/expand sections to focus on specific trades. Drag sections to reorder the entire BOQ structure.
docs.boq_resources_body: Each BOQ position can have linked resources that break down the unit rate into components:
docs.boq_resources_after: To add resources: expand a position row, click "Add Resource", search the catalog, set the quantity factor (e.g., 120 kg rebar per m3 concrete). The position unit rate updates automatically from the sum of its resources.
docs.boq_markups_body: Markups are applied after the net total (sum of all positions). Common markups include:
docs.boq_runval_li1: Positions with errors get a red dot
docs.boq_runval_li2: Positions with warnings get a yellow dot
docs.boq_runval_li3: The validation score (%) appears in the toolbar
docs.boq_runval_li4: Click any dot to see the specific issue and fix it
docs.boq_import_body: The BOQ editor supports importing from multiple sources:
docs.costs_intro: Access the largest open-source construction cost database in the world. The CWICR database contains 55,000+ cost items with descriptions in up to 9 languages, organized by trade (earthworks, concrete, masonry, steel, roofing, facades, HVAC, electrical, plumbing, finishing, landscaping). Each item includes a unit of measurement, base rate for the specified region, classification codes (DIN 276, NRM, MasterFormat), and component breakdown (materials, labor, equipment) when available.
docs.costs_img1_cap: Cost Database with Germany/DACH selected showing 55,719 items with German descriptions, rates, and classification
docs.costs_howto_title: How to Use the Cost Database
docs.costs_howto_body: The cost database is designed for two main workflows:
docs.costs_wfA_title: Workflow A: Browse and Apply to BOQ
docs.costs_wfA_li3: Search for a cost item by typing in the search bar (e.g., "concrete foundation")
docs.costs_wfA_li4: Click the star icon to add frequently used items to your Favorites
docs.costs_wfA_li5: In the BOQ editor, click the database icon next to any position to search and apply a rate
docs.costs_wfB_title: Workflow B: Import Your Own Database
docs.costs_wfB_li2: Prepare an Excel or CSV file with columns: Code, Description, Unit, Rate, Currency
docs.costs_wfB_li3: Upload the file and map columns in the import wizard
docs.costs_wfB_li5: Your imported items appear alongside CWICR data with your custom source tag
docs.costs_vector_body: Enable semantic search to find cost items by meaning, not just exact keyword matches. When you search for "concrete wall", the system also finds items like "reinforced partition C30/37, 24cm with formwork" — even though the exact words do not appear. This is powered by sentence-transformer embeddings that convert descriptions into numerical vectors and find the closest matches in multi-dimensional space. Two vector database backends are supported: LanceDB (embedded, runs in-process like SQLite, 384-dimensional embeddings) and Qdrant (production server with pre-built 3072-dimensional snapshots downloadable from GitHub).
docs.takeoff_intro: Extract quantities directly from your project drawings and models — no manual counting required. Upload Revit, IFC, AutoCAD, or MicroStation files and the system extracts every building element with volumes, areas, and lengths. For PDF drawings, open them directly in the browser and use calibrated measurement tools to take off distances, areas, and element counts. All extracted quantities can be transferred to a BOQ for pricing.
docs.takeoff_img1_cap: PDF Takeoff with 4-step workflow (Upload, AI Analysis, Review, Add to BOQ), project/BOQ selector, and drag-drop upload zone
docs.takeoff_img5_cap: Drop a floorplan, measure distance / area / count with the on-canvas tools, push numbers into BOQ in seconds
docs.takeoff_cadwf_li2: Drag and drop your CAD/BIM file (.rvt, .ifc, .dwg, .dgn) onto the upload area
docs.takeoff_cadwf_li3: Wait for the DDC converter to extract elements (30-60 seconds for typical files)
docs.takeoff_cadwf_li4:
The system shows available columns and suggests grouping presets:
  
Standard Revit QTO — Group by Category + Type Name, sum Volume/Area/Count
Detailed — Add Level for per-floor breakdown
By Family — Group by Revit Family for procurement lists
Custom — Choose any combination of the 1200+ extracted columns
docs.takeoff_cadwf_li6: Review the grouped results — filter out empty groups, delete irrelevant categories, sort by volume
docs.takeoff_pdfwf_li3: The system extracts text and tables from the PDF automatically
docs.takeoff_pdfwf_meas1: Set the drawing scale (click two known points and enter the real distance, or select a preset like 1:100)
docs.takeoff_pdfwf_li6: All measurements are saved and can be exported to a BOQ
docs.ai_intro: Connect any LLM provider to generate construction cost estimates from plain text descriptions, building photos, scanned PDFs, Excel spreadsheets, or CAD/BIM models. The AI system creates complete BOQ positions with quantities and market-rate pricing. After generation, you can match each item against the CWICR cost database to replace AI-estimated rates with verified regional market prices. AI features are entirely opt-in — the platform works fully without them.
docs.ai_img1_cap: AI Estimate page with text input, location/currency/standard selectors, and 5 input modes: Text, Photo, PDF, Excel, Paste
docs.ai_step1_desc: Describe your project in plain text and AI generates 15-30 BOQ items with quantities and market rates.
docs.ai_step2_desc: Match AI estimates against CWICR database to replace guessed rates with real market prices.
docs.ai_step3_desc: Chat with AI about costs, materials, and pricing using your cost database as context.
docs.ai_img3_cap: AI Cost Advisor chat with suggested questions, region selector, and CWICR database integration
docs.planning_intro: Plan your construction project timeline and track costs over time with integrated scheduling and financial modeling tools.
docs.planning_4d_body: The scheduling module provides a professional Gantt chart for managing your construction timeline. Create activities, set durations, define dependencies, and the system automatically calculates the critical path showing which activities must not be delayed.
docs.planning_5d_body: The cost model connects your BOQ data to the project timeline, giving you a time-phased view of construction costs with Earned Value Management metrics.
docs.planning_5d_li1: Gantt chart with drag-and-drop, dependencies (FS/FF/SS/SF), critical path
docs.planning_5d_li3: Earned Value Management: SPI, CPI, EAC, VAC
docs.planning_5d_li4: S-curve (planned vs actual), budget snapshots, cash flow
docs.planning_5d_li5: Monte Carlo simulation for risk-adjusted scheduling
docs.tender_intro: Manage the complete procurement workflow from creating bid packages to comparing proposals and awarding contracts to subcontractors.
docs.tender_create_body: Navigate to Procurement, select a project, and click "Create Package". Specify the package name, scope description, submission deadline, and select which BOQ positions to include. You can create multiple packages per project (e.g., structural works, MEP, finishing).
docs.tender_bids_body: Add bids from subcontractors with company name, contact details, and quoted prices for each BOQ position. The system supports multiple bids per package for side-by-side comparison.
docs.tender_compare_body: The price mirror shows all bids in a comparison table with unit rates and totals per position. Visual indicators highlight the lowest and highest bids. Export the comparison as CSV for stakeholder review.
docs.tender_award_body: Mark the winning bid with the Award button and add justification notes. Package status tracks through: Draft, Issued, Collecting, Evaluating, Awarded, Closed.
docs.risk_intro: Identify, assess, and manage project risks using a probability-impact matrix. Quantify risk exposure and plan mitigation strategies for each identified risk.
docs.risk_matrix_body: The matrix displays all risks on a probability (0.1-0.9) vs impact (low/medium/high/critical) grid with color coding. Each risk shows its calculated exposure score (probability multiplied by impact cost).
docs.risk_manage_body: For each risk, document: unique code (R-001), title, category (technical, financial, schedule, regulatory, environmental, safety), mitigation strategy, contingency plan, risk owner, cost impact, and schedule impact in days. Track status through: Identified, Assessed, Mitigating, Closed, or Occurred.
docs.co_intro: Track scope changes and their impact on project cost and schedule. Change orders provide an audit trail of all modifications made after the initial estimate.
docs.co_workflow_body: Create a change order with code (CO-001), title, and reason category (client request, design change, unforeseen condition, regulatory). Add line items specifying affected BOQ positions with original and new quantities/rates. The system calculates cost deltas automatically. Status moves through: Draft, Submitted, Under Review, Approved/Rejected.
docs.val_img_cap: Validation page - select project and BOQ, run quality checks against configurable rule sets
docs.val_intro: Run automated quality checks on your BOQs to catch errors before they become problems. The validation engine applies 42 configurable rules against your estimate data.
docs.val_results_body: Each validation produces a quality score (0-100%) with color-coded findings: Errors (red, must fix), Warnings (yellow, should review), Info (blue, suggestions). Click any finding to navigate directly to the affected BOQ position.
docs.reports_img1_cap: Reports page with 9 export formats: BOQ (PDF/Excel), Cost Report, GAEB XML, Validation, Schedule, 5D Cost, Tendering, Change Orders, Risk Register
docs.reports_intro: Generate professional documents from your project data for stakeholders, clients, and regulatory compliance.
docs.docmgmt_img_cap: Document management with drag-drop upload, category filters (Drawing, Contract, Specification, Photo), tags, and file preview
docs.docmgmt_intro: Centralize all project documents. Upload drawings, contracts, specifications, and photos with automatic categorization and search.
docs.analytics_img1_cap: Cross-project analytics with KPI cards (Total Budget 125.6M, Variance 63.4M), project comparison table with budget/actual/variance per region
docs.analytics_intro: View cross-project budget performance. The dashboard aggregates financial data across all projects to identify trends, variances, and at-risk budgets.
docs.contacts_intro: Manage all project stakeholders in a centralized directory. Track clients, subcontractors, suppliers, architects, and consultants with full contact details, company associations, and role-based categorization. Link contacts to projects, tenders, purchase orders, and correspondence for full traceability.
docs.tasks_intro: Organize project work with a 5-type task taxonomy and visual Kanban board. Tasks can be assigned to team members, linked to specific BOQ positions, documents, or RFIs, and tracked through customizable workflow stages. Each task type has its own color coding and default fields.
docs.tasks_img_cap: Tasks tied to exact BIM elements — click a task, fly the camera to the element it belongs to. Kanban, list and calendar views all share the same model link.
docs.tasks_ep1: List tasks (filterable by project, assignee, status)
docs.rfi_intro: Track formal information requests between project parties with ball-in-court assignment. RFIs flow through a structured lifecycle from draft to closed, ensuring every question gets a documented answer. Ball-in-court tracking shows who currently owns the action at any time.
docs.finance_intro: Full project financial management covering invoices, budgets, payments, and Earned Value Management (EVM). Track planned vs actual costs, generate payment applications, and monitor budget health with real-time variance analysis. Integrates with BOQ data for automatic cost baseline generation.
docs.procurement_intro: Manage purchase orders and goods receipts for construction materials and services. Create POs linked to BOQ positions, track delivery status, and record goods receipts with quantity verification. Integrates with the Contacts module for supplier management.
docs.safety_intro: Record safety incidents, near-misses, and behavioral observations with a risk scoring system. Track safety performance metrics across projects, identify trends, and ensure compliance with occupational health and safety regulations.
docs.meetings_intro: Manage meeting minutes with structured agendas and tracked action items. Create recurring meeting series (e.g., weekly site meetings), record attendees, capture discussion points, and automatically generate action items that integrate with the Tasks module.
docs.inspections_intro: Conduct quality inspections using configurable checklists. Define inspection templates per trade or activity type, fill them out on-site, attach photos, and track deficiency resolution. Inspection results feed into the project quality score and NCR workflow.
docs.ncr_intro: Document and track quality non-conformances when work does not meet specifications. NCRs follow a formal lifecycle from identification through root cause analysis to corrective action verification, ensuring all quality deviations are properly resolved and documented.
docs.submittals_intro: Manage the submittal review process for construction materials, shop drawings, and product data. Track multi-stage review workflows where submittals pass through multiple reviewers before final approval. Maintain a complete audit trail of all review cycles and revision history.
docs.corr_intro: Maintain a formal communication register for all project correspondence. Track letters, emails, notices, and instructions between project parties with unique reference numbers, response tracking, and full-text search. Ensures contractual communications are properly documented and traceable.
docs.cde_intro: ISO 19650-compliant Common Data Environment for managing project information with controlled status transitions. Documents move through Work in Progress (WIP), Shared, Published, and Archived states with approval gates. Ensures information management follows BIM standards for collaborative delivery.
docs.cde_ep1: List CDE documents (filterable by status, suitability)
docs.transmittals_intro: Formally distribute documents to external parties with tracked delivery and acknowledgment. Create transmittal packages containing multiple documents, specify recipients, and track whether each recipient has received and acknowledged the documents. Provides a contractual record of information exchange.
docs.bimhub_intro: Central hub for BIM model management with element-level data access and direct BOQ linkage. Upload IFC/Revit models, browse building elements with their properties and quantities, and link elements to BOQ positions for 5D cost integration. Provides a bridge between design data and cost estimation.
docs.reporting_intro: Comprehensive reporting engine with a KPI dashboard, customizable report templates, and scheduled report generation. Aggregate data across projects for portfolio-level insights. Create custom report templates combining data from any module (BOQ, Finance, Schedule, Safety, Quality).
docs.notifications_intro: In-app notification system that keeps users informed about events relevant to them. Notifications are triggered by actions across all modules — task assignments, RFI responses, submittal reviews, approval requests, overdue items, and more. Users control their notification preferences per category.
docs.comments_intro: Threaded comment system that can be attached to any entity in the platform — BOQ positions, tasks, RFIs, submittals, documents, inspections, and more. Supports @mentions to notify team members, file attachments, and resolution tracking for action-oriented discussions.
docs.gantt_intro: Pure SVG-based Gantt chart for construction schedule visualization. Renders timelines, dependencies, milestones, and critical path directly in the browser without external libraries. Supports zooming from day-level to year-level views and exports to PDF/PNG for reporting.
docs.regset_intro: Configure timezone, currency, number format, and date format preferences per user and per project. Regional settings cascade from system defaults to project-level overrides to user-level preferences, ensuring each team member sees data formatted according to their locale.
docs.modsys_intro: Enable or disable platform modules to customize OpenConstructionERP for your workflow. Each module can be toggled independently without affecting other modules. Disabled modules are hidden from the UI, their API endpoints return 404, and their database tables remain intact for re-activation.
docs.regpacks_intro: Pre-configured regional packages that set up classification standards, tax rules, cost database defaults, report templates, and regulatory compliance rules for specific markets. Install a regional pack to immediately configure the platform for your country or region.
docs.impexp_intro: Comprehensive data import and export capabilities across all modules. Import contacts, budgets, BOQs, and cost databases from Excel and CSV files with intelligent column mapping. Export project data to Excel, PDF, and CSV with customizable templates. All exports preserve formatting and include metadata headers.
docs.impexp_ep3: Export BOQ (format: excel, pdf, gaeb, csv)
docs.config_img3_cap: Settings page with profile management, Simple/Advanced interface mode toggle, and module configuration
docs.api_modules_footer: See each module section above for detailed endpoint documentation with parameters and descriptions.
docs.usermgmt_intro: Manage team members, roles, and per-module access control.
