docs.video_caption: Captions, chapters and 1.5× playback are built into the player above. Prefer to watch directly on YouTube? ▶ Open on YouTube · subscribe to the channel for new release walkthroughs · download MP4 (47 MB).
docs.see_it_intro: Each block below is a short GIF cut from the full walkthrough above — same order as the video, so you can jump to whichever workflow matters most. Want one continuous flow? ▶ Watch the 12-minute walkthrough on YouTube.
docs.overview_highlights: v3.0.4 highlights: 88 modules covering the full construction lifecycle — Contacts Directory, Tasks with Kanban, RFI tracking, Finance (invoices, budgets, EVM), Procurement, Safety, Meetings, Quality Inspections, NCR, Submittals, Correspondence, CDE (ISO 19650), Transmittals, BIM Hub, Asset Register with COBie export, Reporting & KPI dashboard, Markups (PDF↔DWG↔hub), Pivot visualisations & Charts column picker, Pluggable email service, Multi-tenant Contacts, scheduled reports, 11 Regional Packs, and full Import/Export.
docs.overview_img2_cap: End-to-end walkthrough — Photo → AI estimate → BOQ → BIM/DWG/PDF takeoff → tasks → reports — in one continuous flow
docs.fs_step1_body: Open your browser and navigate to http://localhost:5173 (local development) or http://localhost:8080 (Docker). You will see the login page with a "Demo Access" panel. Click on Admin to log in instantly with the demo account, or enter your own credentials if you registered a new account.
docs.fs_step2_li1: Choose your language — Select from 20 available languages. The entire UI switches immediately.
docs.fs_step2_li2: Select your region — This determines which cost database and classification standard are used by default (e.g., DIN 276 for Germany, NRM for UK, MasterFormat for US).
docs.fs_step2_li3: Configure AI (optional) — If you want AI estimation features, enter an API key from any supported provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, etc.). You can skip this and add it later in Settings.
docs.fs_step2_li4: Load cost database — The system offers to download and import the CWICR cost database for your selected region (55,719 items). This takes about 30 seconds.
docs.fs_step3_body: Click New Project on the Dashboard or navigate to Projects. Fill in:
docs.fs_step3_li3: Region — Determines currency and default classification standard
docs.fs_step3_li4: Classification standard — DIN 276, NRM 1/2, MasterFormat, or custom
docs.fs_step4_body: From the project page, click New Estimate. Give it a name (e.g., "Cost Estimate v1") and optionally choose a template. The BOQ editor opens with an empty grid ready for your first position.
docs.fs_step5_body: In the BOQ editor, click Add Position or press Enter on an empty row. Fill in:
docs.fs_step5_li2: Description — What work is being done (e.g., "Reinforced concrete C30/37 for foundation slab")
docs.fs_step5_li3: Unit — Measurement unit (m3, m2, m, kg, pcs, lsum)
docs.fs_step5_after: The Total column calculates automatically (Quantity x Unit Rate). You can also search the cost database by clicking the database icon next to any position to find and apply a rate from the 55,000+ CWICR items.
docs.install_intro: OpenConstructionERP is designed to be easy to install regardless of your technical background. Choose the method that matches your setup — all options take less than 5 minutes. The application uses SQLite by default, so you do not need to install or configure any database server. Everything works out of the box. Requires Python 3.12+ for the pip and one-line paths.
docs.install_optionA_body: Installs backend + pre-built React frontend in one wheel (~7.4 MB). Opens at http://localhost:8080 with the full UI. SQLite, zero config. If anything looks wrong, run openestimate doctor.
docs.install_linux_body: On Ubuntu 23.04+ (including Ubuntu 26) and Debian 12+, pip install directly to the system Python fails with error: externally-managed-environment (PEP 668). Use a virtual environment instead:
docs.install_linux_headers: If pip falls back to a source build, install the headers: sudo apt install -y build-essential libpq-dev libjpeg-dev zlib1g-dev libgeos-dev. Full Linux guide with troubleshooting: docs/INSTALL_LINUX.md.
docs.install_optionB_body: Picks Docker if installed, otherwise uv, otherwise pip. Runs at http://localhost:8080.
docs.boq_video: 🎬 See the BOQ editor in action on YouTube — keyboard-first editing, 55K+ priced items, live quality score.
docs.boq_img2_cap: Keyboard-first BOQ build — Tab between cells, autocomplete from 55K+ priced items, live quality score, undo/redo every step
docs.boq_img3_cap: Section & position management — drag-and-drop hierarchy, inline edit, AI cost finder, regional rate switching
docs.boq_feat1: Hierarchical structure - Sections, positions, sub-positions with drag-and-drop
docs.boq_feat2: Inline editing - Click any cell to edit. Tab between fields. Undo/redo
docs.boq_feat3: Resources & assemblies - Link labor, materials, equipment. Build reusable recipes
docs.boq_feat4: Markups - Overhead, profit, VAT, contingency per project or regional defaults
docs.boq_feat5: Validation - 42 rules check for missing quantities, zero prices, compliance
docs.boq_feat6: Export - Excel, CSV, PDF report, GAEB XML (X83)
docs.boq_resources_li1: Materials — Concrete, steel, bricks, insulation (from Resource Catalog)
docs.boq_resources_li2: Labor — Worker hours by trade (formworker, electrician, plumber)
docs.boq_markups_li4: VAT/Tax — Value Added Tax (19% in Germany, 20% in UK, varies by country)
docs.boq_markups_after: Click Add Markup in the toolbar. You can set regional defaults or customize per project. Markups can be percentage-based or fixed amounts, and they compound in order (overhead first, then profit on the overhead-inclusive amount, then VAT on the total).
docs.boq_runval_body: Press Ctrl+Shift+V or click the shield icon in the toolbar to run validation. The system checks your BOQ against the selected rule sets and shows results inline:
docs.boq_import_li1: Excel/CSV — Click Import, select your file. The wizard maps your columns (description, quantity, unit, rate) to BOQ fields. Preview before committing.
docs.boq_import_li2: GAEB XML — Import German-standard tender files directly. Positions, sections, and rates are preserved.
docs.boq_import_li3: Paste from clipboard — Copy rows from Excel, click "Paste" in the toolbar. The system detects columns automatically.
docs.boq_import_li4: AI Smart Import — Upload any PDF, photo, or CAD file. AI extracts BOQ positions with quantities and rates.
docs.costs_img2_cap: Instant search across 55K+ priced items in 11 regional databases — type a keyword, unit or classification and apply directly to a BOQ position
docs.costs_wfB_li1: Navigate to Databases → Cost Database → Import Database
docs.costs_struct_li2: Description — Work item name in up to 9 languages
docs.costs_struct_li3: Unit — Unit of measurement (m2, m3, t, pcs, etc.)
docs.costs_struct_li4: Rate — Price per unit in the regional currency
docs.costs_struct_li5: Components — Breakdown into materials, labor, equipment (when available)
docs.costs_struct_li6: Classification — DIN 276, NRM, MasterFormat codes for cross-referencing
docs.takeoff_video: 🎬 Watch the BIM → BOQ bulk-link, DWG layer takeoff and PDF measurement chapters on YouTube.

docs.api_intro: OpenConstructionERP exposes a comprehensive REST API that powers the frontend and can be used for integrations, automation, and custom workflows. Every action available in the UI — creating projects, editing BOQs, running AI estimates, searching costs — is available as an API endpoint. Interactive Swagger documentation with try-it-out buttons is available at /api/docs when running the backend.
docs.pci_intro: AI-powered project analysis that scores your project across 9 weighted domains and generates a completeness score from 0 to 100 (grades A through F).
docs.pci_domain1: BOQ (30%) — Bill of Quantities completeness, pricing, resources
docs.pci_feat1: Critical Gaps — Identifies issues sorted by severity (blocker, critical, warning, suggestion) with one-click fix actions
docs.pci_feat3: AI Recommendations — Connect Anthropic Claude, OpenAI, or Google Gemini in Settings for personalized advice
docs.pci_feat4: Role-based views — Estimator, Manager, and Explorer perspectives
docs.pci_api: API: GET /api/v1/project_intelligence/score/?project_id=X — returns overall score, domain scores, critical gaps, and achievements.
docs.usermgmt_role1: Admin — Full access to all features, user management, system configuration
docs.usermgmt_role2: Manager — Project and team management, approvals, lock/unlock estimates
docs.usermgmt_role3: Editor — Create and edit content across all enabled modules
docs.usermgmt_matrix_body: Admins can configure per-user module visibility and access levels (None, View, Edit, Full) via the /users page. Custom role names can be assigned (e.g., "Site Engineer", "Cost Manager").
docs.usermgmt_feat1: Invite users — Register new team members with role assignment
docs.usermgmt_feat3: Module presets — Full Access, View Only, Minimal quick-apply
